raw:
VULNERABILITY: CWE-416 (Use After Free)
SEVERITY: CRITICAL
CVSS: 9.8
REASON: The product reuses or references memory after it has been freed. At some point afterward, the memory may be allocated again and saved in another pointer, while the original pointer references a location somewhere within the new allocation. Any operations using the original pointer are no longer valid because the memory "belongs" to the code that operates on the new pointer.
label: vuln

raw:
VULNERABILITY: CWE-20 (Improper Input Validation)
SEVERITY: MEDIUM
CVSS: 5.5
REASON: The tipc_sendmsg function in net/tipc/socket.c in the Linux kernel before 4.13.11 does not properly validate the destination address, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (system crash) via a crafted application that sends a message to a non-existent node.
label: vuln

raw:
VULNERABILITY: CWE-20 (Improper Input Validation)
SEVERITY: MEDIUM
CVSS: 5.5
REASON: The tipc_sendmsg function in net/tipc/send.c in the Linux kernel before 4.13.11 does not properly validate the destination address, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (system crash) via a crafted application that sends a message to a non-existent node.
label: vuln

SEVERITY: HIGH
CVSS: 7.5
REASON: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that is intended to identify a file or directory that is located underneath a restricted parent directory, but the product does not properly neutralize special elements within the pathname that can cause the pathname to resolve to a location that is outside of the restricted directory.
label: vuln

VULNERABILITY: CWE-190 (Integer Overflow or Wraparound)
SEVERITY: HIGH
CVSS: 7.5
REASON: The product performs a calculation that can produce an integer overflow or wraparound when the logic assumes that the resulting value will always be larger than the original value. label: vuln

raw:
VULNERABILITY: CWE-416 (Use After Free)
SEVERITY: HIGH
CVSS: 7.8
REASON: The product reuses or references memory after it has been freed. At some point afterward, the memory may be allocated again and saved in another pointer, while the original pointer references a location somewhere within the new allocation. Any operations using the original pointer are no longer valid because the memory "belongs" to the code that operates on the new pointer.
label: vuln

raw:
VULNERABILITY: CWE-476 (NULL Pointer Dereference)
SEVERITY: MEDIUM
CVSS: 5.5
REASON: The product dereferences a pointer that it expects to be valid but is NULL.
label: vuln

raw:
VULNERABILITY: CWE-22 (Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal'))
SEVERITY: HIGH
CVSS: 7.5
REASON: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that is intended to identify a file or directory that is located underneath a restricted parent directory, but the product does not properly neutralize special elements within the pathname that can cause the pathname to resolve to a location that is outside of the restricted directory.
label: vuln
